text: Fill in the blanks to test your understanding of organ function tests and hormones.
questions: The most specific marker of hepatocellular injury in the LFT panel is *ALT/alanine aminotransferase/SGPT*, which is a *cytoplasmic/cytosolic* enzyme found almost exclusively in hepatocytes.
An AST to ALT ratio greater than *2:1/2* suggests *alcoholic/alcohol-related* hepatitis, because alcohol depletes *pyridoxal phosphate/PLP/vitamin B6*.

In primary hypothyroidism, TSH levels are *elevated/high/increased* while free T4 levels are *decreased/low/reduced*, because the feedback loop from the thyroid to the *pituitary/anterior pituitary* is disrupted.

Serum *creatinine/Creatinine* is the most commonly used marker of glomerular filtration rate, while *albumin/Albumin* in the LFT panel reflects hepatic *synthetic/biosynthetic* function with a half-life of approximately *21/twenty-one* days.
